---
title: "Relapse Drift Worksheet 2: Recovery Re-Engagement Plan"
format: html
---
# Relapse Drift Worksheet 2: Recovery Re-Engagement Plan

When drift begins, the most effective response is quick re-engagement with structure and support. Waiting for motivation to return usually increases risk; taking action first often restores motivation.

Re-engagement plans are strongest when they are time-bound and behavior-specific. Clear deadlines and specific actions reduce avoidance and quickly restore momentum after periods of drift.

## Re-Engagement Plan
| Area | What has drifted? | My corrective action | Deadline |
|---|---|---|---|
| Daily schedule |  |  |  |
| Support network |  |  |  |
| Stress management |  |  |  |
| Sleep / physical health |  |  |  |

::: {.callout-tip}
### Try this this week

Use the steps below as a practical experiment and notice what shifts.

Complete at least two corrective actions in the next 48 hours.

Briefly record what worked best so you can keep the strongest strategy.
:::
